sessions.objective: palantir-math Sequencer JSXGraph canvas enhancement: real-time drag intersection tracking, absolute value fold visualization, teaching annotation features
sessions.phase: Phase 2 — Operational Decision-Making: data shapes (JSXGraphScene) exist; adding real-time LOGIC (IntersectionSolver, DependencyGraph) and interactive ACTIONS (DragCascade, BatchBoardUpdate)

pr90Impact.summary: Codex merged translate-source-before-outer-abs semantics. ACTION domain for abs-value drag is NOW PARTIALLY IMPLEMENTED. DATA domain (fold line visualization, ghost curve) and LOGIC domain (FoldGeometry computation for display) remain as gaps.
implementedFiles: functiongraphDragSemantics.test.ts — new tests, runtime-verified via Playwright on problem 15 frame #25

ontologyImpact.obj-jxg-action-05: AbsFoldVisualizationUpdate — fold-AWARE DRAG now implemented in jsxGraphRenderer.ts. REMAINING GAP: fold LINE visualization (ghost curve, reflection guide, fold boundary display) not yet added.
ontologyImpact.obj-jxg-logic-03: DragBehaviorInterface — AbsDragBehavior (translate-source-before-outer-abs) now implemented in functiongraphDragSemantics.ts. OCP confirmed: Linear/Quadratic drag unaffected.
ontologyImpact.obj-jxg-data-06: GraphFamilyProfile — outer-absolute curve recognition added to graphFamily.ts. Family classification now covers Abs outer-wrap pattern.
ontologyImpact.req-jg-02: PARTIALLY SOLVED — drag semantics level complete; VISUAL fold animation and teaching annotation overlay still required.

userArchitecturePreference.direction: Hypothesis A (native-first) is the PREFERRED path. Custom code ONLY for what JSXGraph cannot natively provide: |f(x)| fold visualization, teaching annotations, piecewise/parametric drag semantics.
nativeAPIsToMaximize: board.create('intersection', ...) — native intersection points (v1.12.1 improved precision)
board.create('text', [xFn, yFn, contentFn]) — dynamic coordinate labels
userArchitecturePreference.customLayerScope: Only: |f(x)| ghost curve + fold boundary (JSXGraph has no native abs-fold element), teaching annotation overlay toggle, piecewise drag constraint (no native piecewise drag semantics)

objects.name: IntersectionPoint
objects.description: Resolved (x, y) coordinate pair where two functiongraphs intersect on the JSXGraph canvas. Has identity (which intersection: fg1×fg2 at index 0, 1, …). Created via board.create('intersection', [fg1, fg2, 0]) or JXG.Math.Numerics.root output. Exists as a geometric fact independent of the solver that computed it.
objects.primitive: ObjectType (default — has identity: intersection index + parent pair)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-02: Delete IntersectionSolver (LOGIC) and DragCascadeUpdate (ACTION) — the coordinate pair still exists as a position fact on the canvas → DATA

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'IntersectionPoint' per JSXGraph intersection domain vocabulary. Matches JXG.Intersection element name.
designPrinciples.DRY: Single entity per intersection index — not duplicated per drag frame. Updated in-place.
designPrinciples.OCP: IntersectionPoint structure fixed; solver implementation (NativeIntersection vs Newton) extends IntersectionResolverInterface without modifying this entity.
designPrinciples.PECS: Consumer of two JXG.Curve parents (super), producer of {x, y} coordinate (specific/extends).

objects.forwardPropPath: IntersectionPoint (ontology) → IntersectionCoordinate Struct contract (seq-data.ts) → jsxGraphRenderer.ts board.create('intersection') or root() result → JSXGraphCanvas live coordinate update | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: IntersectionPoint position change → solver iteration count in InteractionTelemetry (lineage) → solver accuracy + precision evaluation → IntersectionResolverInterface warmStart strategy refinement (refinement) | status: partial

objects.name: CoordinateLabel
objects.description: Dynamic text element on JSXGraph canvas displaying the (x, y) coordinates of an IntersectionPoint in real time. Created via board.create('text', [xFn, yFn, contentFn]) where contentFn returns '({x}, {y})' string. The label string is a factual description of a canvas position — independent of the update mechanism.
objects.primitive: ObjectType (default — has identity: which intersection it labels)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-02: Delete AnnotationToggle (ACTION) and update computation (LOGIC) — the label text '(2.00, 1.50)' still describes a canvas fact → DATA

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'CoordinateLabel' per teaching-canvas vocabulary. Maps to JXG.Text element.
designPrinciples.DRY: One label per intersection. Label format (toFixed(2)) declared once in contentFn closure.
designPrinciples.OCP: Label format extensible (add slope, degrees) via contentFn update without modifying CoordinateLabel lifecycle or IntersectionPoint entity.
designPrinciples.PECS: Consumer of IntersectionPoint position (super/abstract position), producer of formatted string (specific/extends).

objects.forwardPropPath: CoordinateLabel (ontology) → TextElementContract: { xFn: ()=>number, yFn: ()=>number, contentFn: ()=>string } → board.create('text') in jsxGraphRenderer.ts → JSXGraphCanvas SVG text node | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: CoordinateLabel visibility events → annotation usage in TeachingEffectiveness (lineage) → teaching effectiveness evaluation → label position/format refinement | status: partial

objects.name: FunctiongraphScene
objects.description: The JSXGraph board scene document: all declared elements (functiongraphs, points, sliders, text, auxiliary curves), their configuration params, and auto-intersection metadata. Persisted as JSXGraphScene type in seq-data.ts. The canonical DATA artifact of the canvas renderer.
objects.primitive: ObjectType (default — has identity: scene ID / board ID)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-02: Delete all computation — the scene document (element declarations, config) still exists as a fact in JSXGraphCanvas.tsx → DATA

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'FunctiongraphScene' per JSXGraph canvas domain vocabulary. Extends existing JSXGraphScene type.
designPrinciples.DRY: Single scene document per board. No duplication of element declarations.
designPrinciples.OCP: New element types added to scene without modifying scene identity or serialization contract.
designPrinciples.PECS: Producer of all board elements — scene is the source of truth for canvas state.

objects.forwardPropPath: FunctiongraphScene (ontology) → JSXGraphScene type in seq-data.ts → jsxGraphRenderer.ts board initialization → JSXGraphCanvas board.create() calls | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: Scene mutation events → scene version in InteractionTelemetry → scene drift evaluation → JSXGraphScene type contract refinement | status: partial

objects.name: DragState
objects.description: Struct value type: the current translation vector { ty: number, tx?: number } applied to a dragged functiongraph. ty is the vertical displacement from the original position. No identity — just the current delta value. Describes the positional reality of a dragged function at a given frame.
objects.primitive: Struct (value type — no identity, just the delta)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-02: Delete DragCascadeUpdate (ACTION) and DragSemanticResolver (LOGIC) — ty=2.5 still describes the current position reality → DATA

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'DragState' per drag interaction domain vocabulary. ty follows existing functiongraphDragSemantics.ts convention.
designPrinciples.DRY: Single ty value per function. No duplication across snapshot frames — DragStateReducer aggregates.
designPrinciples.OCP: DragState Struct extensible (add tx for horizontal drag) without modifying reducer or solver logic.
designPrinciples.PECS: Producer of ty/tx delta — consumed by IntersectionSolver and AbsValueFoldComputer as input.

objects.forwardPropPath: DragState (ontology) → DragState type in seq-data.ts → functiongraphDragSemantics.ts translation computation → functiongraph curve function update | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: DragState change → ty delta in InteractionTelemetry.dragFrames (lineage) → drag responsiveness evaluation → DragBehaviorInterface snap/constraint refinement | status: partial

objects.name: FoldGeometry
objects.description: Struct value type: the geometric description of an absolute value fold visualization. { ty: number, foldBoundaryXValues: number[], reflectionGuidePoints: [number, number][], absMaxY: number }. Describes what the fold LOOKS LIKE at a given ty — independent of the computation that derived it.
objects.primitive: Struct (value type — no identity, just the fold description)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-02: Delete AbsValueFoldComputer (LOGIC) and AbsFoldVisualizationUpdate (ACTION) — the fold boundary x-values and reflection points still describe a geometric reality → DATA

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'FoldGeometry' per absolute value teaching visualization vocabulary.
designPrinciples.DRY: Single struct per |f(x)+ty| expression. All four visual layers (primary, abs, ghost, boundary) share this struct.
designPrinciples.OCP: FoldGeometry extensible (add fold color, annotation text) without modifying AbsValueFoldComputer.
designPrinciples.PECS: Producer of fold geometry description — consumed by AbsFoldVisualizationUpdate and CoordinateLabel.

objects.forwardPropPath: FoldGeometry (ontology) → FoldGeometry type in seq-data.ts → jsxGraphRenderer.ts abs rendering → AbsValueFoldVisualization ghost curves + fold boundary line | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: FoldGeometry change → fold boundary x-value in InteractionTelemetry → fold accuracy evaluation → AbsValueFoldComputer intercept precision refinement | status: partial

objects.name: GraphFamilyProfile
objects.implementationStatus: partial — PR #90 merged: outer-absolute curve recognition added to graphFamily.ts. family: 'Abs' (outer-wrap) now correctly classified. REMAINING: Piecewise, Parametric classification not yet implemented.
objects.description: Classification result per functiongraph: { family: 'Linear'|'Quadratic'|'Cubic'|'Quartic'|'Abs'|'Piecewise'|'Parametric', dragModel: string, intersectionSupport: boolean }. Produced by graphFamily.ts classification. Has identity (which functiongraph it describes). PR #90: outer-absolute (Abs) family now recognized. Piecewise/Parametric still needed.
objects.primitive: ObjectType (has identity — one profile per functiongraph element)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-02: Delete DragSemanticResolver (LOGIC) — the classification label 'Quadratic' still describes a mathematical fact about the graph → DATA

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'GraphFamilyProfile' per graphFamily.ts domain vocabulary. Extends existing GraphFamily type.
designPrinciples.DRY: Single profile per functiongraph. DragBehaviorInterface implementations reference profile without duplicating classification logic.
designPrinciples.OCP: New families (Piecewise, Parametric) extend GraphFamilyProfile.family union without modifying existing classification.
designPrinciples.PECS: Producer of family classification — consumed by DragBehaviorInterface as input parameter.

objects.forwardPropPath: GraphFamilyProfile (ontology) → GraphFamilyProfile type extension in seq-data.ts → graphFamily.ts classifier output → functiongraphDragSemantics.ts family-aware drag dispatch | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: GraphFamilyProfile misclassification → classification error in InteractionTelemetry → accuracy evaluation → graphFamily.ts classifier refinement | status: partial

objects.name: TracePath
objects.description: Struct value type: recorded array of (ty, timestamp) positions during a functiongraph drag session. { draggedElementId: string, frames: { ty: number, tx?: number, timestamp: number }[] }. The drag history fact — independent of the recording mechanism.
objects.primitive: Struct (value type — no identity, just the path record)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-02: Delete TraceCurveRecord (ACTION) — the recorded path array still describes the drag history reality → DATA

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'TracePath' per drag-history domain vocabulary.
designPrinciples.DRY: Single array per drag session. TraceCurveRecord appends to it without duplicating the path structure.
designPrinciples.OCP: TracePath extensible (add velocity field) without modifying recording action or curve.dataX/dataY binding.
designPrinciples.PECS: Producer of drag history — consumed by TraceCurveRecord (ACTION) for curve update, by InteractionTelemetry (LEARN) for evaluation.

objects.forwardPropPath: TracePath (ontology) → TracePath type in seq-data.ts → board.on('update') push logic → curve.dataX/dataY binding → JSXGraphCanvas trace overlay | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: TracePath data → drag path in InteractionTelemetry (lineage) → teaching effectiveness evaluation (did trace help?) → TracePath resolution/density refinement | status: partial

objects.name: IntersectionResolverInterface
objects.description: DC5-06 Interface primitive: polymorphic contract for computing IntersectionPoint coordinates from two functiongraphs. Two implementations: (A) NativeIntersectionResolver — board.create('intersection', [fg1, fg2, 0]) using JSXGraph's v1.12.1 improved algorithm; (B) NewtonRaphsonResolver — JXG.Math.Numerics.root(h, warmStart) where h(x)=f(x)-g(x). Enables OCP: swap solvers without modifying IntersectionPoint entity or DragCascadeUpdate action.
objects.primitive: Interface (shared behavioral contract — NativeIntersection vs NewtonRaphson implementations)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-01: HOW TO REASON about intersection coordinates from two curves. Defines the computation contract, not the stored result.

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'IntersectionResolverInterface' per JSXGraph intersection domain + DC5-06 Interface vocabulary.
designPrinciples.DRY: One interface — two implementations. Zero code duplication: NativeIntersection and NewtonRaphson share the same call site.
designPrinciples.OCP: New solver strategies (e.g., Bisection, Secant) added as new implementations without modifying the interface or IntersectionPoint entity.
designPrinciples.PECS: Consumes [JXG.Curve, JXG.Curve] (super — abstract curve type), produces IntersectionCoordinate (extends — {x: number, y: number}).

objects.forwardPropPath: IntersectionResolverInterface (ontology) → IntersectionResolverContract interface in seq-data.ts → jsxGraphRenderer.ts: board.create('intersection') or JXG.Math.Numerics.root() adapter → board.update() → IntersectionPoint auto-refresh | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: Resolver output + iteration count → solver performance in InteractionTelemetry (lineage) → precision + latency evaluation (<1ms budget) → warmStart strategy + algorithm selection refinement | status: partial

objects.name: AbsValueFoldComputer
objects.description: Pure function: derives FoldGeometry from f_raw(x: (x: number) => number, ty: number) → FoldGeometry. Computation: (1) absY(x) = Math.abs(f_raw(x) + ty), (2) foldBoundaryX: solve f_raw(x) + ty = 0 via JXG.Math.Numerics.root, (3) reflectionGuide: sample y = -(f_raw(x) + ty) for negative region, (4) absMaxY: max of absY on visible range. All four visual layers share ty closure.
objects.primitive: Function (pure computation — input: f_raw + ty, output: FoldGeometry, no side effects)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-01: HOW TO REASON about |f(x)+ty| fold geometry. SH-03: returns FoldGeometry (derived value) without committing any canvas mutations → LOGIC not ACTION

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'AbsValueFoldComputer' per absolute value teaching visualization vocabulary.
designPrinciples.DRY: Single ty-sharing closure across all four visual layers (primary, abs, ghost, boundary). Zero duplication of fold logic.
designPrinciples.OCP: FoldGeometry output extensible without modifying AbsValueFoldComputer core algorithm.
designPrinciples.PECS: Consumes (f_raw: Function, ty: number) — super/broad inputs. Produces FoldGeometry — specific/extends output.

objects.forwardPropPath: AbsValueFoldComputer (ontology) → FoldComputerContract: (f, ty) => FoldGeometry (contract) → jsxGraphRenderer.ts abs curve function definition → AbsValueFoldVisualization ghost curves | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: Fold boundary x-values → x-intercept accuracy in InteractionTelemetry → fold precision evaluation → root-finding strategy refinement | status: partial

objects.name: DragBehaviorInterface
objects.implementationStatus: partial — PR #90 merged: AbsDragBehavior (translate-source-before-outer-abs) now implemented in functiongraphDragSemantics.ts. OCP validated: Linear/Quadratic drag unaffected. REMAINING: PiecewiseDragBehavior, ParametricDragBehavior, and outer-abs VISUAL fold update not yet connected to ghost curve rendering.
objects.description: DC5-06 Interface primitive: per-family drag semantic contract. { execute(event: DragEvent, profile: GraphFamilyProfile, board: JXG.Board): DragState }. Implementations per family: LinearDragBehavior (translate ty only), QuadraticDragBehavior (translate ty, snap to grid), AbsDragBehavior [PR #90: translate-source-before-outer-abs IMPLEMENTED], PiecewiseDragBehavior (segment-aware — STILL NEEDED). Directly extends functiongraphDragSemantics.ts.
objects.primitive: Interface (shared behavioral contract — one implementation per GraphFamilyProfile)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-01: HOW TO REASON about drag semantics per family. Defines the drag behavior contract, not the stored delta.

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'DragBehaviorInterface' per DC5-06 Interface vocabulary and drag semantics domain.
designPrinciples.DRY: One interface — four implementations. No drag logic duplication across Linear/Quadratic/Abs/Piecewise.
designPrinciples.OCP: New graph families (Parametric) added as new DragBehaviorInterface implementations without modifying existing behaviors.
designPrinciples.PECS: Consumes DragEvent (super — all drag events), produces DragState (extends — family-specific translation result).

objects.forwardPropPath: DragBehaviorInterface (ontology) → DragBehaviorContract in functiongraphDragSemantics.ts → family dispatch in drag handler → canvas functiongraph position update | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: DragBehavior execution → family-specific drag metric in InteractionTelemetry → per-family accuracy evaluation → DragBehaviorInterface implementation refinement | status: partial

objects.name: DependencyGraph
objects.description: DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph) of JSXGraph element → its dependent elements for cascade update ordering. Each node = a board element; each edge = 'A depends on B' (A must update when B changes). This is the REASONING STRUCTURE that determines what must be recomputed when a drag frame fires. In Hypothesis A: this IS JSXGraph's internal _dep system. In Hypothesis B: this is an explicit custom graph.
objects.primitive: ObjectType (logic domain — a reasoning artifact with identity: the graph for a given board)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-01: HOW TO REASON about element dependency ordering. The graph answers 'what must update when X drags?' — pure reasoning, no mutations.

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'DependencyGraph' per JSXGraph internal _dep system vocabulary (board element dependency graph).
designPrinciples.DRY: Single graph per board. Not duplicated per element type.
designPrinciples.OCP: New dependent element types extend the graph by adding edges — no modification of existing graph reasoning.
designPrinciples.PECS: Consumes board element set (super), produces update ordering (specific DAG traversal result).

objects.forwardPropPath: DependencyGraph (ontology) → DependencyGraph reasoning contract → jsxGraphRenderer.ts element creation order → board._dep internal structure (H-A) or explicit observer map (H-B) | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: Graph traversal events → dependency cascade log in InteractionTelemetry → cascade correctness evaluation → graph topology refinement | status: partial

objects.name: DerivativeEvaluator
objects.description: Pure function: computes numerical slope value at a given x position using JSXGraph's native Derivative element or finite difference. { evaluate(curve: JXG.Curve, x: number): number }. Used to display slope at intersection and critical points for teaching.
objects.primitive: Function (pure — input: curve + x, output: slope number, no side effects)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-01: HOW TO REASON about slope at a point. SH-03: returns derived number without committing canvas mutations → LOGIC

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'DerivativeEvaluator' per mathematical analysis vocabulary. Binds to JXG 'derivative' element (board.create('derivative', [curve])).
designPrinciples.DRY: Single evaluator; derivative display element created once and auto-updates via native dependency.
designPrinciples.OCP: Derivative display extensible (add second derivative) without modifying evaluator contract.
designPrinciples.PECS: Consumes JXG.Curve (super), produces slope: number (specific).

objects.forwardPropPath: DerivativeEvaluator (ontology) → DerivativeContract: (curve, x) => number → board.create('derivative', [curve]) in jsxGraphRenderer.ts → derivative curve auto-display | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: Slope display events → derivative usage in TeachingEffectiveness (lineage) → teaching utility evaluation → DerivativeEvaluator precision refinement | status: partial

objects.name: DragCascadeUpdate
objects.description: ACTION: fires on every drag frame (board.on('update') or element.on('drag')) and triggers the full dependent element recomputation chain: (1) resolve new IntersectionPoints via IntersectionResolverInterface, (2) update CoordinateLabel text, (3) update FoldGeometry via AbsValueFoldComputer if functiongraph has |f(x)| shape, (4) push to TracePath, (5) fire BatchBoardUpdate for SVG repaint. Changes canvas state when executed.
objects.primitive: ObjectType (Action Type — DC5-06 default for action domain objects)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-03: Commits edits — triggers canvas mutations (element position updates, label text updates, SVG repaint) → ACTION

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'DragCascadeUpdate' per drag interaction domain vocabulary.
designPrinciples.DRY: Single cascade action per drag frame. All N dependent elements updated within one board.on('update') or parent-point drag handler.
designPrinciples.OCP: DragCascadeUpdate closed to new element types — extending DependencyGraph is sufficient to add new dependents.
designPrinciples.PECS: Not directly applicable to action types.

objects.forwardPropPath: DragCascadeUpdate (ontology) → DragCascadeContract: (event: DragEvent) => void → board.on('update') or parentPoint.on('drag') handler → canvas element position mutations | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: DragCascadeUpdate execution → frame timestamp + element update counts in InteractionTelemetry (lineage) → frame rate + cascade latency evaluation → BatchBoardUpdate strategy refinement | status: partial

objects.name: BatchBoardUpdate
objects.description: ACTION: board.suspendUpdate() → mutate N elements → board.unsuspendUpdate() → single SVG repaint. Prevents N intermediate DOM reflows during cascade update. Critical for 60fps with 10+ interactive elements. isSuspendedRedraw flag prevents partial renders.
objects.primitive: ObjectType (Action Type — DC5-06)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-03: board.unsuspendUpdate() commits all buffered mutations in a single SVG repaint — produces DOM side effects → ACTION

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'BatchBoardUpdate' per JSXGraph board API vocabulary (suspendUpdate/unsuspendUpdate).
designPrinciples.DRY: Single batch pattern — all N element mutations within one suspend/unsuspend block. No individual element.update() calls.
designPrinciples.OCP: New elements added to batch without modifying suspend/unsuspend call sites.
designPrinciples.PECS: Not applicable to action types.

objects.forwardPropPath: BatchBoardUpdate (ontology) → BatchUpdateContract: { suspend(): void; unsuspend(): void } → board.suspendUpdate()/unsuspendUpdate() in jsxGraphRenderer.ts → single SVG repaint | status: healthy
objects.backwardPropPath: BatchBoardUpdate execution → repaint timing in InteractionTelemetry (lineage) → repaint latency evaluation → batch grouping strategy refinement | status: partial

objects.name: AnnotationToggle
objects.description: ACTION: activate/deactivate teaching overlay elements (CoordinateLabels, derivative display, fold boundary) by setting opacity function on JSXGraph text elements: { opacity: function(){ return teachingMode ? 1 : 0; } }. Changes element visibility state on canvas — a canvas reality mutation.
objects.primitive: ObjectType (Action Type — DC5-06)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-03: Sets opacity on declared board elements — applies a canvas visibility mutation → ACTION

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'AnnotationToggle' per teaching-mode domain vocabulary.
designPrinciples.DRY: Single opacity function pattern shared across all annotation elements. teachingMode flag is the single control point.
designPrinciples.OCP: New annotation elements added to teaching overlay without modifying AnnotationToggle logic.
designPrinciples.PECS: Not applicable to action types.

objects.forwardPropPath: AnnotationToggle (ontology) → AnnotationContract: { toggle(mode: boolean): void } → opacity function update in board elements → JSXGraphCanvas visibility change | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: AnnotationToggle events → annotation state in TeachingEffectiveness (lineage) → teaching effectiveness evaluation → annotation strategy refinement | status: partial

objects.name: TraceCurveRecord
objects.description: ACTION: on each drag frame, append { ty, timestamp } to TracePath.frames array AND update the trace display curve's curve.dataX / curve.dataY arrays + call curve.update(). Produces a visual 'ghost trail' of where the function has been. Note: JSXGraph native Tracecurve requires Glider — this custom approach uses a plain Functiongraph curve with mutable dataX/dataY.
objects.primitive: ObjectType (Action Type — DC5-06)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-03: Appends to TracePath array AND mutates curve.dataX/dataY — produces persistent state side effects → ACTION

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'TraceCurveRecord' per drag-history visualization vocabulary.
designPrinciples.DRY: Single record-and-update loop per drag frame. TracePath is the single source of truth for the trace data.
designPrinciples.OCP: TracePath extensible (add velocity) without modifying TraceCurveRecord core append logic.
designPrinciples.PECS: Not applicable to action types.

objects.forwardPropPath: TraceCurveRecord (ontology) → TraceContract: { record(frame: TraceFrame): void } → board.on('update') push logic → curve.dataX/dataY mutation → JSXGraphCanvas trace curve render | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: TracePath data → drag path in InteractionTelemetry (lineage) → trace density evaluation → TraceCurveRecord sampling rate refinement | status: partial

objects.name: AbsFoldVisualizationUpdate
objects.implementationStatus: partial — PR #90 merged: fold-AWARE DRAG implemented in jsxGraphRenderer.ts (translate-source-before-outer-abs). REMAINING: fold LINE visualization (ghost curve y=-(f_raw(x)+ty), reflection guide, fold boundary x-line) not yet rendered as separate board elements.
objects.description: ACTION: When DragState.ty changes, recompute FoldGeometry via AbsValueFoldComputer and update the four canvas elements: (1) primary f(x)+ty curve via shared ty closure [PR #90: DONE], (2) abs curve Math.abs(f_raw(x)+ty) via shared ty closure [PR #90: DONE], (3) reflection guide dashed curve y=-(f_raw(x)+ty) for negative region [STILL NEEDED], (4) fold boundary x-line position [STILL NEEDED]. All four share the same ty reference — a single ty update triggers all four via BoardUpdate.
objects.primitive: ObjectType (Action Type — DC5-06)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-03: Updates four canvas board elements — commits fold geometry mutations when executed → ACTION

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'AbsFoldVisualizationUpdate' per absolute value fold teaching vocabulary.
designPrinciples.DRY: Single ty closure shared across all four curve functions — zero duplication of fold state.
designPrinciples.OCP: New fold visual layers (add CurveIntersection region shading) without modifying ty closure or fold computer.
designPrinciples.PECS: Not applicable to action types.

objects.forwardPropPath: AbsFoldVisualizationUpdate (ontology) → FoldUpdateContract: (ty: number) => void → jsxGraphRenderer.ts curve function updates → ghost curve + boundary line board elements | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: Fold update events → fold display timing in InteractionTelemetry → fold render latency evaluation → ty closure update strategy refinement | status: partial

objects.name: InputSanitization
objects.description: XSS trust boundary: LaTeX → JS function compilation via new Function(body) in latexToJSXGraph.ts. Governs which LaTeX strings may be compiled to executable JavaScript. Critical boundary: user-supplied LaTeX expressions become live code. Must validate that compiled functions don't escape the mathematical evaluation context (no DOM manipulation, no fetch calls).
objects.primitive: ObjectType (Permission/Security boundary — DC5-06)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-04: Governs WHO (which LaTeX expressions) may cross the trust boundary into executable JavaScript → SECURITY

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'InputSanitization' per security boundary vocabulary. Maps to latexToJSXGraph.ts compile-time validation.
designPrinciples.DRY: Single compilation path — all LaTeX expressions go through one sanitization point (200-entry LRU cache).
designPrinciples.OCP: Validation rules extensible (add new forbidden patterns) without modifying compilation pipeline.
designPrinciples.PECS: Not applicable to security boundaries.

objects.forwardPropPath: InputSanitization (ontology) → LaTeXSanitizationContract: { compile(latex: string): Result<Function, Error> } → latexToJSXGraph.ts compile-time check → jsxGraphRenderer.ts safe function usage | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: Compilation errors → error pattern in InteractionTelemetry (lineage) → XSS surface evaluation → sanitization rule refinement | status: partial
objects.provenanceNote: [Official] — LaTeX → new Function() pattern confirmed in latexToJSXGraph.ts (src/lib/latexToJSXGraph.ts). XSS risk is an inference from new Function() usage.

objects.name: InteractionTelemetry
objects.description: LEARN audit record capturing: (1) drag events (elementId, ty delta, timestamp), (2) intersection discoveries (IntersectionPoint coords, solver iterations, appeared/disappeared), (3) frame timing (dragFrame duration, repaint duration), (4) annotation usage (which CoordinateLabels visible, toggle events). Used by evaluator for frame rate evaluation, solver accuracy evaluation, and teaching effectiveness evaluation.
objects.primitive: ObjectType (Audit Record — DC5-06 LEARN default)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-05: Captures WHAT HAPPENED (drag events), WHEN (timestamps), THROUGH WHICH action (DragCascadeUpdate), WHAT WAS LEARNED (frame rate, solver accuracy) → LEARN

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'InteractionTelemetry' per canvas interaction observability vocabulary.
designPrinciples.DRY: Single telemetry record per session. DragCascadeUpdate, BatchBoardUpdate, and IntersectionResolver all append to the same record via event.type discriminator.
designPrinciples.OCP: New event types (fold updates, annotation toggles) extend LogEntry schema without modifying telemetry capture logic.
designPrinciples.PECS: Not applicable to audit types.

objects.forwardPropPath: InteractionTelemetry (ontology) → TelemetryContract: { record(event: TelemetryEvent): void } → telemetry capture hook in board.on('update') → evaluation pipeline input | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: Telemetry records → lineage entries (lineage) → frame rate + solver precision + annotation utility evaluation → DragBehaviorInterface / IntersectionResolverInterface / CoordinateLabel refinement proposals | status: partial

objects.name: TeachingEffectiveness
objects.description: LEARN feedback signal: { sessionId, annotationsUsed: string[], totalDragFrames: number, intersectionDiscoveries: number, teachingModeEngagements: number, studentAttentionHeuristic: 'high'|'medium'|'low' }. Aggregates session-level signals about which teaching features drove engagement. Informs CoordinateLabel placement, annotation density, and fold visualization design refinements.
objects.primitive: ObjectType (Feedback Signal — DC5-06 LEARN default)
objects.semanticHeuristic: SH-05: Captures WHAT WAS LEARNED from teaching usage (which features engaged students) — pure LEARN feedback, not a canvas state fact → LEARN

designPrinciples.DDD: Named 'TeachingEffectiveness' per educational feedback domain vocabulary.
designPrinciples.DRY: Single aggregate per session. InteractionTelemetry is the raw log; TeachingEffectiveness is the derived summary.
designPrinciples.OCP: New effectiveness metrics extensible without modifying existing feedback schema.
designPrinciples.PECS: Not applicable to feedback signals.

objects.forwardPropPath: TeachingEffectiveness (ontology) → TeachingFeedbackContract → post-session aggregation pipeline → CoordinateLabel/AnnotationToggle refinement proposals | status: partial
objects.backwardPropPath: TeachingEffectiveness signal → LEARN refinement proposal (refinement) → CoordinateLabel placement + AnnotationToggle strategy update (ontology update) → forward prop restart | status: partial
objects.provenanceNote: [Inference] — no direct evidence source for teaching effectiveness metric design. Derived from §ARCH-05 LEARN loop requirement and TeachingAnnotation gap in decision-log.

forwardPropPath.description: ontology → contracts → backend renderer → frontend canvas
steps: IntersectionResolverInterface + IntersectionPoint (ontology) → IntersectionResolverContract type + IntersectionCoordinate Struct in seq-data.ts (contract) → jsxGraphRenderer.ts: board.create('intersection') or JXG.Math.Numerics.root() adapter (backend renderer) → JSXGraphCanvas board.update() live IntersectionPoint display (frontend canvas)

DragBehaviorInterface + DragState (ontology) → DragBehaviorContract extension in functiongraphDragSemantics.ts (contract) → family dispatch in element.on('drag') or parentPoint drag handler (backend renderer) → canvas functiongraph position update (frontend canvas)

AbsValueFoldComputer + FoldGeometry (ontology) → FoldGeometry type + FoldComputerContract in seq-data.ts (contract) → jsxGraphRenderer.ts abs curve + ghost curve + fold boundary (backend renderer) → AbsValueFoldVisualization canvas elements (frontend canvas)

DragStateReducer primitive (ontology) → DragStateContract: { reduce(events): DragState } in functiongraphDragSemantics.ts (contract) → board.on('update') ty accumulation (backend renderer) → canvas drag state (frontend canvas)
gaps: IntersectionResolverContract interface not yet declared in src/types/seq-data.ts — STILL NEEDED
DragBehaviorInterface formal contract not yet added to functiongraphDragSemantics.ts (PR #90 adds AbsDragBehavior impl but not the typed interface) — STILL NEEDED
FoldGeometry Struct type not yet declared in seq-data.ts — STILL NEEDED (fold ghost curve + boundary not yet rendered)
TeachingEffectiveness telemetry capture hook not yet declared in jsxGraphRenderer.ts — STILL NEEDED
[PR #90 CLOSES] AbsDragBehavior ACTION path: translate-source-before-outer-abs implemented in functiongraphDragSemantics.ts + jsxGraphRenderer.ts
[PR #90 CLOSES] GraphFamilyProfile outer-abs classification: graphFamily.ts updated

backwardPropPath.description: drag events → interaction lineage → evaluation → refinement → ontology updates
steps: DragCascadeUpdate ACTION fires → drag frame timestamp + ty delta + element update count → InteractionTelemetry.dragFrames (lineage) → frame rate + cascade latency evaluation → DragBehaviorInterface implementation refinement (refinement) → DragBehaviorInterface algorithm note update (ontology)

IntersectionResolverInterface output → solver iteration count → InteractionTelemetry.intersectionEvents (lineage) → warmStart effectiveness + solver precision evaluation → IntersectionResolverInterface algorithm strategy refinement (refinement) → implementation guide update (ontology)

AnnotationToggle ACTION fires → annotation visibility state → TeachingEffectiveness.annotationsUsed (lineage) → teaching effectiveness evaluation → CoordinateLabel placement/format refinement (refinement) → CoordinateLabel DATA entity update (ontology)
gaps: InteractionTelemetry capture hook not yet implemented in board.on('update') handler
Evaluation function schemas for frame rate + solver precision not yet specified (blocked on T6 simulator)
TeachingEffectiveness aggregation pipeline not yet declared

primitives.name: IntersectionResolverInterface
primitives.rationale: Two competing solver implementations (NativeIntersection vs NewtonRaphson) share one call site. OCP: swap solvers without modifying IntersectionPoint entity or DragCascadeUpdate action.

primitives.name: DragBehaviorInterface
primitives.rationale: Per-family drag semantics (Linear/Quadratic/Abs/Piecewise) share one dispatch call. OCP: new families as new implementations. DDD: named per graphFamily.ts domain vocabulary.

primitives.name: IntersectionCoordinate
primitives.description: { x: number, y: number } — value type output of IntersectionResolverInterface. No identity, just the coordinate pair.
primitives.rationale: DC5-06 Struct for compound value output. No identity needed — IntersectionPoint ObjectType has identity.

primitives.name: FoldGeometry
primitives.rationale: DC5-06 Struct for fold shape description. Four visual layers (primary, abs, ghost, boundary) all share this one value type without requiring identity.

primitives.name: DragStateReducer
primitives.description: DC5-06 Reducer: canonical focus value = current DragState (ty, tx); preserved history = TracePath.frames. Collapses drag frame stream into current authoritative drag state.
primitives.rationale: Exactly models DC5-06 'collapse multi-value property histories into canonical focus value while preserving history'. Drag frames are history; current ty is the canonical focus.

primitives.name: intersectionCoords
primitives.description: Derived from [fg1, fg2] current functiongraph state → IntersectionCoordinate[]. Auto-derives as parent functiongraph positions change.
primitives.rationale: DC5-06 Derived Property: computed from other ontology entities, not stored independently. In H-A: JSXGraph native _dep system handles this. In H-B: explicit re-derivation in board.on('update').

primitives.name: foldBoundaryX
primitives.description: Derived from f_raw + ty → x[] where f_raw(x)+ty=0. Computed by AbsValueFoldComputer as part of FoldGeometry.
primitives.rationale: DC5-06 Derived Property: foldBoundaryX values are derived from functiongraph parameters, not stored. Change when ty changes via drag.

primitives.name: FunctiongraphToIntersection
primitives.description: Links FunctiongraphScene elements (source) to IntersectionPoint entities (target) with payload: { index: number, solverType: 'native'|'newton', warmStartX: number }.
primitives.rationale: DC5-06 Object-Backed Link Type: the relationship between a functiongraph and its intersection points has data (solver type, index) that belongs on the link itself, not on either endpoint entity.

designPrinciplesApplied.DDD: All 15 objects named per domain vocabulary: JSXGraph API names (IntersectionPoint, CoordinateLabel, BatchBoardUpdate, Tracecurve), mathematical vocabulary (FoldGeometry, AbsValueFoldComputer, DerivativeEvaluator), DC5-06 primitive names (DragBehaviorInterface, DragStateReducer). No invented abstractions. DDD partially violated in Hypothesis B architecture ('board observer' is non-standard vocabulary — flagged in hypothesis).
designPrinciplesApplied.DRY: Rule of three applied: (1) IntersectionResolverInterface abstracts NativeIntersection + NewtonRaphson — prevents two separate solver call sites. (2) ty closure shared across four AbsFoldVisualization curve functions. (3) DragBehaviorInterface prevents per-family duplication in drag handler. DRY NOT yet triggered for CoordinateLabel (only 1 label type so far).
designPrinciplesApplied.OCP: All 5 Interfaces/Structs declared as extension points: IntersectionResolverInterface (add new solvers), DragBehaviorInterface (add new families), FoldGeometry (add new visual layers), DependencyGraph (add new element edges), TeachingEffectiveness (add new metrics). Confirmed for all 15 objects.
designPrinciplesApplied.PECS: Applied to IntersectionResolverInterface: consumes [JXG.Curve, JXG.Curve] (super — abstract curve type), produces IntersectionCoordinate (extends — specific {x,y} result). Applied to DragBehaviorInterface: consumes DragEvent (super), produces DragState (extends). Applied to AbsValueFoldComputer: consumes (f_raw: Function, ty: number) (super), produces FoldGeometry (extends). Not directly applicable to action types or audit records.

securityConcerns.concern: InputSanitization: new Function() compilation in latexToJSXGraph.ts creates XSS attack surface — malicious LaTeX input could compile to DOM-manipulating JS
securityConcerns.mitigation: Existing 200-entry LRU cache. Should add explicit allowlist validation of compiled function output before execution.
